assertions.expectation: Explains the GKE version skew policy: nodes must be within 2 minor versions of the control plane
assertions.reason: Clearly states GKE only supports nodes within 2 minor versions of the control plane and identifies this as a version skew violation

assertions.expectation: Identifies that 1.28 nodes with a 1.31 control plane violates the skew policy (3 minor versions)
assertions.reason: Explicitly states nodes are 3 minor versions behind the control plane (1.28 vs 1.31) which exceeds the maximum supported skew

assertions.expectation: Warns about potential issues: kubelet incompatibility, API features not working, possible workload disruption
assertions.reason: Lists specific issues including API calls may fail, newer Kubernetes features unavailable, kubectl commands may not work properly, and workload instability

assertions.expectation: Recommends sequential node pool upgrades (1.28→1.29→1.30→1.31) not a direct jump
assertions.reason: Explicitly outlines upgrade path through 1.29 → 1.30 → 1.31 with sequential minor versions recommended, and provides three-phase upgrade plan

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for the node pool upgrade steps
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud container node-pools upgrade commands for each phase of the upgrade process

assertions.expectation: Recommends using conservative surge settings (maxSurge=1, maxUnavailable=0) given the risky state
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends configuring conservative surge settings with max-surge-upgrade 1 and max-unavailable-upgrade 0

assertions.expectation: Advises monitoring closely between each version bump for issues
assertions.reason: Includes validation steps after each upgrade phase and provides commands to check node status and workload health

assertions.expectation: Mentions that future prevention includes setting up release channel enrollment and maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Discusses prevention strategies including keeping node pools on auto-upgrade, using maintenance windows instead of disabling upgrades, and monitoring version skew
